results.title: Welcome to Television (1956)
results.brief_description: Gyngell is often credited as being the first person to appear on Australian television on 16 September 1956, when he spoke the words, ‘Good evening, and welcome to television’.

constellations_other.name: Lost and found
constellations_other.description: Blossoming from Alice Guy Blaché’s The Cabbage-Patch Fairy, this Constellation explores the impermanence and enduring nature of the moving image.

results.title: Welcome back, Norman
results.creator_credit: Shevard Goldstein, Sharon Burke, Film Communications and Mt Lemmon Production
results.brief_description: The comical character, Norman Krasner, is presented once again in this sequel of “Norman Krasner”. As a character who can turn common misfortune into near disaster, Norman depicts the weary businessman returning from a business trip. The setting is a busy airport’s parking lot. Norman turns the simple experience of getting out of the parking lot into a hilarious example of “Murphy’s Law”. Cast: Doug MacIntosh.

results.title: Weight training for sport
results.creator_credit: Bill Latto and Town and Country Productions
results.brief_description: The National Coach to the British Amateur Weight-lifting Authority, Mr Al Murray, instructs pupils at a London gymnasium in weight training techniques. Demonstrates how the correct exercises can increase athletic proficiency in all fields of sporting endeavour.

results.title: Weight training for everybody
results.creator_credit: Henri Bagdadi and Universal Education and Visual Arts
results.brief_description: Shows a weight-training class, which includes both men and women ranging in age from 12 to 40. The instructor emphasises the need for adequate warm-up exercises such as running in place, jumping jacks, and push-ups. After each exercise is performed, animation shows the location and movement of the muscles involved and the use of those muscles is related to everyday activity.

results.title: Weekly review. Ep. 382. This is the weather office
results.creator_credit: J.M.D. Hardwick, Stanhope Andrews and New Zealand National Film Unit
results.brief_description: The work of the weather office of New Zealand where, with the assistance of 120 reporting stations in New Zealand and on surrounding islands and reports from ships at sea, meteorological information is collated and from it the weather forecast is given.

results.title: Weekly review. 187th week. The Pilchard fishermen
results.brief_description: The pilchard fishermen in New Zealand’s Marlborough Sound haul their nets by night. At dawn carrier pigeons fly from the boats to the canning factory with reports of the size of the catch, so that when the boats come in all is ready for processing the fish.

results.title: Wednesdays [Widescreen]
results.creator_credit: Jeremy Stanford and Victorian College of the Arts, School of Film and Television
results.brief_description: Shelly (Josephine Byrnes) awakens one Wednesday morning and slowly descends down the stairs in her pajamas. As the morning progresses the viewer learns of love found, lost, jealousy, suspicion and a murder as she retraces the steps of how she discovered her partner, John’s (Greg Stone) infidelity - or does she?

results.title: Wednesday in April
results.brief_description: In image and sound, the film describes an average day as it is lived across the varied scene of the continent of America. Cowboy, farmer or urban worker, every citizen is free to weave his own unique pattern into the infinite, multi-colored American tapestry. Narrated by Lee Marvin.
